title: Mattress freshener spray
subtitle: A bicarbonate-of-soda and lavender spray for mattresses, pillows, and soft furnishings
excerpt: Bicarbonate of soda neutralises acidic odour compounds rather than masking them; lavender and eucalyptus add a clean scent. Shakes together in five minutes and leaves mattresses smelling fresh without fragrance chemicals.
sourceNotes: Bicarbonate of soda as an odour neutraliser: standard household chemistry. Lavender antimicrobial and scent properties: Cavanagh and Wilkinson, Phytotherapy Research (2002).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep each bottle to 250 ml so the bicarbonate concentration stays correct.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh every four weeks; bicarbonate-of-soda solutions can develop a faint stale smell over time.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within one month. Discard if the spray develops cloudiness or a sour smell. Shake before each use.

glossaryTerms.definition: Sodium bicarbonate -- a mild alkali that neutralises acidic odour compounds. Distinct from washing soda, which is a much stronger alkali.

content.text: to neutralise stale smells in mattresses, pillows, and fabric headboards. It works by reacting with acidic odour molecules rather than covering them up. The lavender and eucalyptus contribute a clean scent and a mild antimicrobial action.

Pour 250 ml of warm distilled water into the spray bottle or a measuring jug. Add 2 tablespoons of bicarbonate of soda and swirl or stir until fully dissolved. Warm water speeds up dissolution; cold water works but takes longer.
Add 20 drops of lavender and, if using, 10 drops of eucalyptus. Essential oils do not dissolve in water; they disperse in fine droplets when the bottle is shaken. Shake well before each use.
Pour into a 250 ml trigger-spray bottle and label with the date. An amber glass bottle protects the essential oils from light degradation.

Shake well. Hold the bottle 30 cm from the mattress surface and apply a light, even mist. Do not saturate -- a fine mist is enough. Leave the mattress uncovered for 30 to 60 minutes in a ventilated room before remaking the bed. Safe for cotton, linen, and synthetic mattress fabrics; test a hidden area of delicate fabrics first.

Keep at room temperature, away from direct sunlight. Use within one month. Discard if the spray develops cloudiness or a stale, flat smell -- bicarbonate-of-soda solutions degrade over time and oxidising essential oils lose both their scent and their antimicrobial properties.
